title: crepis newii subsp. oliveriana
intro: Crepis newii subsp. oliveriana is a subspecies of the Crepis newii plant, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It is native to the mountainous regions of South Africa and has been used traditionally for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and treating respiratory issues, due to its flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones content. Crepis newii subsp. oliveriana is often used in herbal preparations, including infusions and decoctions, to leverage its medicinal properties.
